title: Bothriocline fusca

The genus Bothriocline, to which Bothriocline fusca belongs, is primarily native to tropical regions of Africa. The classification of Bothriocline fusca is further supported by its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower structure.

intro: Bothriocline fusca, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, which include treating inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, as well as exerting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bothriocline fusca include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
